payload.message:
Create Cory’s morning briefing and EMAIL it to cory.stoner@icloud.com from cory.stoner@icloud.com using Himalaya (account: icloud). Do NOT send to WhatsApp.

CRITICAL RELIABILITY REQUIREMENT (sanitization):
- BEFORE embedding any external command output (especially icalBuddy) into a heredoc/JSON/shell arg, sanitize it to remove NUL bytes and other control characters.
- Strip \x00 and all chars with ord < 32 except \n, \r, \t. (Backspace \b and NUL \x00 previously broke the send step.)
- Recommended sanitizer:
  python3 - <<'PY'
  import sys
  s=sys.stdin.read().replace('\\x00','')
  s=''.join(ch for ch in s if ch in '\\n\\r\\t' or (ord(ch)>=32 and ord(ch)!=127))
  sys.stdout.write(s)
  PY

Data sources (use local commands):
- Calendar (today): use icalBuddy and include ONLY these calendars: Work, Appointment/Meeting, Errands/Tasks, Family, Events, Work Shift Schedule.
  Example:
  icalBuddy -b '' -nc -ic 'Work,Appointment/Meeting,Errands/Tasks,Family,Events,Work Shift Schedule' -iep 'title,datetime,location,url,notes' eventsToday
  Then pipe through sanitizer above.
- Reminders:
  - Due today: remindctl today --json
  - Overdue: remindctl overdue --json
- Weather: current + today forecast for Cory’s area (America/New_York). Prefer wttr.in; if it times out, use api.weather.gov.

Add NEWS (topics: tech, AI, local Pittsburgh):
- Find 1–2 interesting, high-quality articles.
- If web_search is unavailable, use reliable RSS (e.g., https://www.theverge.com/rss/index.xml) and/or Post-Gazette RSS (http://www.post-gazette.com/rss) and include direct links.
- For each: Title — Outlet — 1 sentence why it matters — link.

Add RELEVANCE:
- 1–3 bullets of anything else that could be fun/useful/informative today based on available signals.

Email formatting:
- Subject: Morning Briefing — {YYYY-MM-DD}
- Body: multipart/alternative with BOTH plain text and simple HTML.

HTML style:
- Clean system font stack; section headers; bullet lists; subtle separators.
- No external images.

Send via: himalaya message send --account icloud with proper MIME headers + boundaries.

After sending: verify success (exit code / output) and if send fails, surface an error (do not silently succeed).

payload.text:
TASK_PACKET v1
Owner: ORION
Requester: ORION
Objective: Perform a daily system status check on the OpenClaw gateway and active sessions.
Success Criteria:
- System status report generated.
- No critical errors identified in gateway or session lists.
Constraints:
- Do not perform any system modification actions.
- Keep analysis to OS/Gateway/Session level status checks.
Inputs:
- None
Risks:
- Low. Read-only checks.
Stop Gates:
- Any command that modifies system state, files, or credentials.
Output Format:
- Bulleted summary of check results.

Execute the Inputs commands/scripts, then write a Result: block under that packet in tasks/INBOX/ATLAS.md using the packet's Output Format. If no eligible pending packet exists, respond NO_REPLY.

jobs.name: routing-audit-weekly
jobs.description: Weekly POLARIS routing/queue audit summary via ORION Telegram

payload.message:
TASK_PACKET v1
Owner: ORION
Requester: ORION
Objective: Run weekly routing audit and post a concise Telegram line-item summary.
Success Criteria:
- Runs scripts/routing_audit_weekly.py once.
- Returns exactly five summary lines in the required format.
Constraints:
- Summary only; no raw logs or command dumps.
- No destructive actions.
Inputs:
- scripts/routing_audit_weekly.py
- tasks/INBOX/POLARIS.md
- docs/AGENT_OWNERSHIP_MATRIX.md
Risks:
- low
Stop Gates:
- Any credential/config mutation.
Output Format:
- Return exactly these 5 lines and nothing else:
  Queue: <active>/8, oldest=<Nd>
  Aging: >24h=<n>, >48h=<n>, >72h=<n>, >120h=<n>
  Escalations: <none|brief items>
  Routing: misroutes=<n>, gate_misses=<n>, notify_noise=<n>
  Next actions: <none|1-3 brief bullets>

Run first:
cd /Users/corystoner/src/ORION && python3 scripts/routing_audit_weekly.py

Mapping rules:
- queue=<a>/8 oldest=<d>d -> Queue line
- aging_bands=>24h:x,>48h:y,>72h:z,>120h:w -> Aging line
- misroutes_or_requester_violations, kalshi_gate_misses, notification_noise -> Routing line
- If misroutes>0 or gate_misses>0 or any aging band >72h >0: Escalations must not be none.
- If misroutes>0 or gate_misses>0 or notify_noise>0 or any aging band >24h >0: Next actions must include 1-3 concrete bullets (not none).
- Only when all routing metrics are zero and all aging bands are zero: Escalations and Next actions may both be none.

jobs.name: arc-field-guide-forced-handoff-loop
jobs.description: Forces 3 offline specialist delegations (SCRIBE+LEDGER+POLARIS) with ORION handoff packets

payload.message:
TASK_PACKET v1
Owner: ORION
Requester: ORION
Objective: Force offline specialist progress for Arc Raiders Field Guide using ORION-built handoff context packets.
Success Criteria:
- Complete exactly 3 specialist delegations in this run:
  - SCRIBE for FG-Chapters-Layout (edit target: `docs/arc-raiders-field-guide/chapters-layout-draft.md`)
  - LEDGER for FG-Cost-Breakdown (edit target: `docs/arc-raiders-field-guide/cost-breakdown-draft.md`)
  - POLARIS for FG-Project-Management (edit target: `docs/arc-raiders-field-guide/project-management-draft.md`)
- Child specialists must operate from ORION-provided handoff context only (no Discord reads).
- Return concrete draft deltas against target drafts (section edits, row edits, add/remove proposals).
- ORION must apply at least one concrete edit to each target draft file every run, using specialist output or direct fallback synthesis.
- Post one FG-HQ rollup with session IDs, attempted/completed counts, and actionable edit deltas.
Constraints:
- Timebox: 16 minutes max.
- Concurrency: max 3 sub-agent sessions.
- Use `sessions_spawn` with `mode="run"` only.
- No destructive/config/credential actions.
- Do not skip delegations due to "no material deltas".
- Delegation gate: if fewer than 3 delegations are attempted, write `last_error="forced_handoff_not_executed"` to `tmp/arc_fg_loop_state.json` and report blocked status.
- Child instruction hard rule: include `NO_DISCORD_TOOLS` in each child task and provide inline handoff context packet with:
  - target thread id/name
  - latest message excerpts (up to 12)
  - objective and done criteria
  - known blockers
  - relevant links from `docs/arc-raiders-field-guide/sources.md`
- Wait for all child runs to complete before final summary.
- Completion gate: do not return final summary until all child session IDs are captured and each target file has a proposed or applied delta.
- Direct-edit fallback: if any specialist output is partial/blocked, ORION must still update that target file directly with best-effort concrete edits and mark fallback used.
- Strict completeness rule: final rollup is valid only if all required markers below are present and set to `yes`.
- Blocking rule: if any required marker is missing or `no`, set `last_error="forced_handoff_incomplete_rollup"` in `tmp/arc_fg_loop_state.json` and include `BLOCKED_REASON: forced_handoff_incomplete_rollup` in rollup.
Inputs:
- tmp/arc_fg_thread_registry.json
- tmp/arc_fg_loop_state.json
- docs/arc-raiders-field-guide/sources.md
- docs/arc-raiders-field-guide/chapters-layout-draft.md
- docs/arc-raiders-field-guide/cost-breakdown-draft.md
- docs/arc-raiders-field-guide/project-management-draft.md
- docs/TASK_PACKET.md
Output Format:
- FG-HQ rollup with:
  - delegations attempted/completed
  - child session IDs
  - draft deltas from SCRIBE, LEDGER, and POLARIS
  - links/sources referenced
  - next 3 edits to apply
  - literal markers (required):
    - SCRIBE_DELTA_PRESENT: yes|no
    - LEDGER_DELTA_PRESENT: yes|no
    - POLARIS_DELTA_PRESENT: yes|no
    - CHAPTERS_FILE_UPDATED: yes|no
    - COST_FILE_UPDATED: yes|no
    - PM_FILE_UPDATED: yes|no
    - FINAL_STATUS: complete|blocked

jobs.name: quest-ps5-world-news-update
jobs.description: Weekday PS5 world news update via QUEST (sourced, concise).

payload.message:
TASK_PACKET v1
Owner: QUEST
Requester: ORION
Objective: Deliver a concise PS5 gaming world news update.
Success Criteria:
- Provides 4-7 high-signal PS5-related updates from recent coverage.
- Each bullet includes source domain and publication date.
- Ends with one short "Watch next" line.
Constraints:
- Retrieval-first: use web retrieval tools and source-backed items; no fabrication.
- Focus on PS5 ecosystem: first-party announcements, major third-party PS5 updates, release-date shifts, platform/firmware updates, and major incidents.
- If no high-signal updates are found, say so explicitly and provide up to 2 watch items.
- FINAL OUTPUT MUST NOT BE EMPTY.
Inputs:
- Preferred source domains: blog.playstation.com, playstation.com, ign.com, gamespot.com, eurogamer.net, videogameschronicle.com.
Risks:
- medium: stale or low-signal headlines.
Stop Gates:
- Any paid action, account mutation, or irreversible operation.
Output Format:
- Title line: PS5 World News Update (<date>)
- 4-7 bullets max, each ending with source domain + date in parentheses.
- Final line: Watch next: <short item(s)>.
- If retrieval fails or yields nothing, output exactly this fallback (and nothing else):
  PS5 World News Update (<date>)
  - No high-signal PS5 updates found in the latest scan window. (n/a)
  Watch next: Next scheduled scan.

Critical:
- After any tool call, you MUST return final plain-text content.
- Do not end the turn with an empty response.

jobs.name: orion-monthly-scorecard-daily
jobs.description: Daily monthly scorecard refresh for 2026-03

payload.message:
Use system.run to execute exactly:
cd /Users/corystoner/src/ORION && MONTH=$(date +%Y-%m) && make monthly-scorecard MONTH="$MONTH"

Rules:
1) Wait for completion. If system.run returns a running session id, keep polling that session until it exits.
2) If the command exits non-zero, reply exactly: ERROR monthly_scorecard_rc_nonzero
3) After exit code 0, verify freshness by running:
   cd /Users/corystoner/src/ORION && export MONTH=$(date +%Y-%m) && python3 - <<'PY'
import os, time
month = os.environ['MONTH']
p = f'eval/monthly-scorecard-{month}.md'
if not os.path.exists(p):
    raise SystemExit(2)
age = time.time() - os.path.getmtime(p)
print(int(age))
raise SystemExit(0 if age <= 1800 else 3)
PY
4) If freshness check fails, reply exactly: ERROR monthly_scorecard_stale_artifact
5) If all checks pass, reply exactly: NO_REPLY

jobs.name: orion-stop-gate-daily

payload.message:

1) Wait for completion. If system.run returns a running session id, keep polling that session until it exits.
2) If the command exits non-zero, reply exactly: ERROR stop_gate_nonzero
3) After exit code 0, verify freshness by running:
   cd /Users/corystoner/src/ORION && python3 - <<'PY'
import glob, os, time
json_files = sorted(glob.glob('eval/history/stop-gate-*.json'))
md_files = sorted(glob.glob('eval/history/stop-gate-*.md'))
if not json_files or not md_files:
    raise SystemExit(2)
latest_json = json_files[-1]
latest_md = md_files[-1]
age = max(time.time() - os.path.getmtime(latest_json), time.time() - os.path.getmtime(latest_md))
print(latest_json)
print(latest_md)
print(int(age))
raise SystemExit(0 if age <= 900 else 3)
PY
4) If freshness check fails, reply exactly: ERROR stop_gate_stale_artifact
5) If all checks pass, reply exactly: NO_REPLY

jobs.name: Memory Dreaming Promotion
jobs.description: [managed-by=memory-core.short-term-promotion] Promote weighted short-term recalls into MEMORY.md (limit=10, minScore=0.800, minRecallCount=3, minUniqueQueries=3, recencyHalfLifeDays=14, maxAgeDays=30).

payload.message: __openclaw_memory_core_short_term_promotion_dream__
